originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&__thisopt_814` is never used.

sanitizedDescription: The value written to `&thisopt` is never used.
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 1a3c8e3d840.c L13: 'thisopt = SSL_PROTOCOL_TLSV1_1;' inside 'if (rand() % 2)' — thisopt is assigned but never subsequently used (function returns without reading thisopt). Genuine dead store. SSL_PROTOCOL_TLSV1_1 is only defined when HAVE_TLSV1_X=T (L4), and HAVE_TLSV1_X=F is non-compilable (per VBDB rules for this file), so only HAVE_TLSV1_X=T is analyzed. PC: !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc (STRICT_ANSI and __USE_EXTERN_INLINES cancel). !_STDLIB_H is restrictive — bug also occurs in normal compilation. No HAVE_TLSV1_X in PC but it's implicitly T (only compilable config). New bug, over-constrained by restrictive !_STDLIB_H.
